title: The End We Sought
content:
The end we sought is not attained,
    But wisdom has been won,
    And thus a higher goal is gained.
    That like the moon has sadly waned,
    While this shines as the sun.

    A shorter route to India's strand
    Columbus failed to find.
    That was an object truly grand,
    But in the wealth of this fair land
    Grandeur and good combine.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Joseph Horatio Chant, titled "The End We Sought", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
